data.text/markdown:
MotherDuck 🧡 marimo

Throughout this notebook, we will explore using MotherDuck inside marimo. If you’re new to marimo, check out our GitHub repo: marimo is free and open source. You can expand the code of any cells to see how the output are being created.
Let's attach a remote MotherDuck database using md:

console.name: stdout
console.text:
Attempting to automatically open the SSO authorization page in your default browser.
Please open this link to login into your account: https://auth.motherduck.com/activate?user_code=NMPN-PHGJ


Token successfully retrieved ✅

You can display the token and store it as an environment variable to avoid having to log in again:
  PRAGMA PRINT_MD_TOKEN;

data.text/markdown: !!! tip "Explore data sources" If you open the "Explore data sources" panel on the left side bar (3rd icon), you will see all your tables including any news ones we will create below
Let's make some queries 🦆

data.text/markdown:
Let's make some charts 📈

Now that we have made some queries and named the results, we can chart those resulting dataframes in Python, using our favorite charting libraries (e.g altair, matplotlib, or plotly).

Adding reactivity ⚡

We can also parameterize our SQL using marimo UI elements. This not only makes our SQL reactive, but also any downstream logic, including our charts.
